root_motivation: Provide one canonical Rust crate that every component of the MQO fleet (binder, compilers, router, server, benchmark) imports for the shared Multidimensional Query Object schema — eliminating per-component type drift and the unconstrained SQL string output space.
user_persona: AtScale MQO fleet engineers and LLM agent code that constructs queries; they import this crate and rely on its JSON Schema to validate MQO payloads at runtime.

unfakeable_metric.name: tests_pass_count
unfakeable_metric.harness_command: cargo test --release --workspace 2>&1 | grep -E 'test result' | awk '{sum += $4} END {print sum}'

acceptance_criteria.description: All MQO types round-trip through JSON losslessly (serde_json to-string → from-string equals original) for every fixture.
mqo-spec emits a valid JSON Schema document for Mqo (validated with schemars in a test).
validate() rejects: empty measures, limit of 0, and a Range filter with lo > hi — each with a distinct MqoError variant.
At least 6 golden fixtures (one per TimeIntel variant + one calc-group filter + one member filter) parse and validate.
cargo test --release passes; cargo clippy -- -D warnings is clean.
acceptance_criteria.test: CI: cargo test --release && cargo clippy -- -D warnings exits 0

acceptance_criteria.description: BoundMqo carries resolved unique_names + per-ref metadata flags (is_calc, semi_additive, required_dimension).

scope: Typed serde Serialize/Deserialize Rust structs for Mqo, MeasureRef, LevelSelection, Filter, TimeIntel, OrderKey, BoundMqo
Filter enum variants: Member, Range, CalcGroupMember
TimeIntel enum variants: YoY, PriorPeriod, ToDate(Grain), RunningTotal, Share, Rank
schemars-derived JSON Schema for Mqo emitted via a build artifact or binary sub-command
validate(&Mqo) -> Result<(), Vec<MqoError>> — structural validation only
Golden JSON fixtures under tests/fixtures/ covering all TimeIntel variants plus filter types
non_goals: No query execution, parsing, or compilation logic — schema and validation only
No network or async runtime dependency
No semantic/model validation (e.g. checking whether a measure name exists in a model)
No binder implementation — BoundMqo is a type stub only

five_whys_trace.q: Why define a separate crate for the MQO schema rather than inline it in the binder or compiler?
five_whys_trace.a: Each fleet component currently invents its own representation of an MQO, causing type drift between the binder, compilers, router, server, and benchmark.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does type drift matter if each component works correctly in isolation?
five_whys_trace.a: When the binder hands off to a compiler, the payload crosses a serialization boundary — if the types disagree, the JSON round-trip silently drops or misinterprets fields.

five_whys_trace.q: Why not just share the JSON Schema without a Rust crate?
five_whys_trace.a: Non-Rust producers (the LLM skill, other languages) can validate against the JSON Schema, but Rust consumers need a typed struct to avoid manual serde boilerplate that re-drifts from the schema.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is this the right moment to ship it rather than deferring until a second component needs it?
five_whys_trace.a: The query-semantic-layer skill's 13-rule gate (R1–R13) exists specifically because free-text SQL has an unconstrained output space; the MQO is the closed structure that replaces it, and every downstream component that ships without this crate re-embeds the unconstrained assumption.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should BoundMqo be a full binder implementation or a type stub?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Type stub only — the binder implementation is a separate PRD/crate.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Is the JSON Schema emitted at build time (build.rs) or at runtime?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Both acceptable; implementation choice is to emit via a test + schemars::schema_for! macro, with optional bin helper.
